key_insight: A plasma proteomics + machine learning approach identified LAG3, NPC2, and LAMP3 as top predictors of rheumatoid arthritis–associated interstitial lung disease (RA-ILD) risk.
clinical_relevance: This work suggests a potential route to earlier RA-ILD risk stratification, which could help prioritize closer monitoring of high-risk patients.
method: Used UK Biobank data to test whether rheumatoid arthritis is linked to incident ILD risk.
Screened 2,920 plasma proteins in 781 RA patients for ILD-associated signals.
Built and compared eight machine learning models, then used SHAP to interpret the best-performing model.
evidence_signal: RA was associated with higher ILD risk in Cox analysis, Mendelian randomization supported a causal link, and CatBoost achieved the best discrimination with AUC 0.884 (95% CI 0.773-0.996).
watch_item: The biomarker candidates and model performance need independent external validation before use in practice, especially because the sample size for proteomic modeling was modest.
paper_title: Development of a Novel Risk Prediction Model for Rheumatoid Arthritis-Associated Interstitial Lung Disease (RA-ILD): A Longitudinal Study
